rag_rules: Latched expressions must be used only on input data ports or event expressions over input event data ports in component implementations.
Binary 'and' expressions require both operands to be of type bool; fix type mismatches by correcting operand types or expressions.
AGREE diagnostics pointing to implementation-level references in type annexes should be resolved by moving valid bindings to implementation scope or removing invalid clauses.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and use only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Assumptions and guarantees should be scoped to the appropriate AADL component type or implementation level.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
When AGREE reports type errors, trace the root cause to unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations and fix them directly.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must respect AADL scoping rules.
Boolean expressions in AGREE require operands of type bool; numeric comparisons must be explicitly cast or handled correctly.
Helper functions and constants in AGREE must be declared within the same annex or imported from valid packages.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or visible features.
Do not attempt to resolve type errors by adding new architecture elements; instead, correct or remove the problematic AGREE clause.
Do not assume that AADL property names like 'Period' or 'Deadline' are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics related to annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
Model fusion or component insertion location decisions
Component type structural extraction or feature definitions
